title: MeshNodeViewerScene with Orbit Camera and Automation Endpoints
description: Create the foundational viewer scene that all subsequent mesh node work validates against. This is the FIRST ticket to build -- without it, no other ticket has a way to visually or programmatically validate its output. The scene renders a hardcoded test cube using raw GL calls (same pattern as IcosphereRuntime -- VAO/VBO/EBO with 8-float interleaved vertices). Camera orbits the mesh center via left-click drag. Scroll zooms. The scene also exposes mesh state to the AutomationRuntime.uiState() so the CLI can query vertex/face counts and bounding box for automated validation. Later tickets (MESH-4) will replace the hardcoded cube renderer with the generalized HalfEdgeMeshRuntime.
definition-of-done: 1. MeshNodeViewerScene exists at ixdar-app/src/main/java/ixdar/scenes/mesh/MeshNodeViewerScene.java with @SceneAnnotation(id='mesh-viewer'). 2. Camera orbits the mesh center on left-click drag (azimuth + elevation). 3. Scroll wheel zooms in/out. 4. Scene renders a hardcoded unit cube with correct lighting via MeshShader. 5. VS Code launch config and Maven profile ('mesh-viewer') are generated. 6. Scene compiles and launches without errors via 'mvn -P mesh-viewer'. 7. AutomationRuntime.uiState() includes a 'mesh' section when this scene is active, with vertexCount, faceCount, boundingBoxMin, boundingBoxMax. 8. 'python ixdar_cli.py health' returns ok when the scene is running. 9. 'python ixdar_cli.py screenshot' captures the rendered cube.
testing-plan: 1. Launch via 'mvn -P mesh-viewer', verify cube appears. 2. Left-click drag to orbit, verify smooth rotation around cube center. 3. Scroll to zoom, verify camera distance changes. 4. Run 'python ixdar_cli.py health', verify ok. 5. Run 'python ixdar_cli.py ui-state', verify mesh section with vertexCount=8, faceCount=12. 6. Run 'python ixdar_cli.py screenshot --out tmp/mesh-test.png', verify image shows a cube. 7. Run 'python ixdar_cli.py shutdown', verify clean exit.
todos: DONE : Run scenes-new-scene.md CLI to scaffold MeshNodeViewerScene with --base Scene --camera 3d --id mesh-viewer --maven-profile mesh-viewer
DONE : Create OrbitMouseTrap extending MouseTrap: azimuth, elevation, distance fields
DONE : Wire mouseDragged to update azimuth/elevation, recompute camera position on sphere around target
DONE : Wire scroll to adjust orbit distance
DONE : In initGL(), create a hardcoded unit cube: 8 vertices, 12 triangles, VAO/VBO/EBO upload (same 8-float layout as MeshShader expects)
DONE : In drawScene(), set MeshShader uniforms and draw the cube with drawElements
DONE : Store mesh stats (vertexCount, faceCount, bboxMin, bboxMax) as fields on the scene
DONE : Add a 'mesh' section to AutomationRuntime.uiState() when canvas is MeshNodeViewerScene
DONE : Add shutdown() that cleans up GL resources
DONE : Verify via automation CLI: health, ui-state, screenshot, shutdown
unknowns: Whether to add a grid floor and axis indicators for spatial reference in the initial version
Whether middle-click should pan the orbit target in addition to left-click orbit
changes-made: Scaffolded MeshNodeViewerScene plus matching VS Code launch config and Maven mesh-viewer profile.
Implemented OrbitMouseTrap for left-drag orbit and scroll-based zoom around a target point.
Rendered a hardcoded unit cube through MeshShader using scene-owned VAO/VBO/EBO buffers and cleanup hooks.
Extended AutomationRuntime.uiState() with mesh vertex/face counts and bounding box payloads for the mesh viewer scene.
Removed premature @MeshNodeAnnotation registration from Icosphere so annotation processing no longer breaks the build.
